title: Hide Account Dropdown When User Has Only One Account
story: As a DataTools Pro user with only one account, I want the account dropdown in the top panel to be hidden, so that the interface is clean and I am not confused by an unnecessary control.
description:
The account switcher dropdown in the top navigation panel should be hidden when a user has no shared accounts — i.e., they only belong to a single account. This applies to users who signed up independently before being invited into a team organization.

## Behavior
- If the user has only one account (no shared/team accounts), the dropdown is hidden entirely
- If the user has multiple accounts (personal + shared org), the dropdown is displayed as normal
acceptance_criteria: Account dropdown is hidden in the top panel when the user belongs to only one account
Account dropdown is visible when the user has access to multiple accounts
Users who signed up before being invited to a team see the dropdown only after team invitation
ux_changes: Account switcher dropdown hidden from the top panel when user has only one account. Reduces visual clutter for solo users.
middleware_changes: Server-side check added to determine the count of accounts associated with the user before rendering the dropdown.
implementation_notes: Conditional rendering based on account count. The dropdown is only rendered when the user has more than one account (personal + shared). This handles the edge case of users who signed up independently before being invited to a team organization.
comment_summary: Developer implemented and product owner confirmed it looks clean.
